title: Five Money-Aligned Statements That Don't Sound Mad Out Loud
subtitle: A set of energy statements for someone new to this kind of practice
excerpt: Five short energy statements for someone who has been told to say affirmations about money and found them embarrassing or unconvincing. Each one is grounded, present tense, and specific enough to feel usable rather than abstract.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. The release-and-allow framework is from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you're new to money work and want a set of statements that feel usable rather than embarrassing to say aloud.

content.text: Five energy statements for someone who is new to this practice, or who has tried affirmations before and found the standard versions too big, too vague, or too much to say with a straight face. These are grounded, specific, and present tense.

Pick one. Say it three times in the morning, hand on chest. Move to a different one tomorrow if the first one doesn't fit. None of them require you to believe they're true yet — saying them is the first step toward that.
Money can sit in my account without me panicking about it.
I am allowed to earn more than I currently do.
I look at the number from steady ground.
My money situation is workable and I am working on it.
Written for homemade.education. The energy statement format draws on the release-and-allow framework in The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
